title: Goblin (solo RPG) and Korg Pirates! - Small, quick, and easy games
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Nhb3_wu7Ddk

Daniel (@dungeondive) brings you a double dose of micro games perfect for stealing a few minutes during a hectic day. Goblin is a delightful solo hex crawl where you play, well, a goblin hunting for crown fragments across a charmingly weird landscape—complete with witches who turn you into toads and NPCs whose teeth are apparently better than currency. The art is wonderfully goofy, and there's genuine humor lurking in every encounter.

But if Goblin's your appetizer, Pirates Corg is the espresso shot—a three-card wonder that somehow packs naval adventure into something wallet-sized. Choose your locations, roll your d6, and pray your crew doesn't mutiny. Both games excel at delivering surprising depth without demanding much table space or your precious time.

Daniel also showcases his legendary micro-game collection, crammed into a box so perfectly organized it borders on obsessive (in the best way). If you've been sleeping on coffee-break gaming, these lightweight adventures might just be the gateway drug.

----

What's your sweet spot for game length? Do you prefer a ten-minute escape or something that demands a proper evening commitment?
